title: Executable filesystem coverage guard - dsl + ir/domain files must be in dep map

evidence.ref: Audit confirmed dsl/ and ir/domain/ are in sync today but nothing executable enforced it. ssotdeps verify only checks declared paths exist on disk (forward direction); the inverse (existing files are declared) was open. Same recurring drift class as D033 workflow-tool coverage, applied to source directories
033-register-ci-tools - same inverse-coverage pattern, applied to .github/workflows -> tools/ rather than filesystem -> dep map

guards.ref: internal/ssotdeps.VerifyFileSystemCoverage - walks dsl/**/*.lisp and ir/domain/*.ir.json; asserts each file's repo-relative path is registered in m.SsotArtifacts. Wired into ssotdeps cross-check as the 6th sub-check axis
concept-map C-021 - codifies the rule; concept-map bumped v0.25.0 to v0.26.0

examples: Pre-D038: nothing prevented adding dsl/bogus.lisp without a dep map entry; the file would be invisible to D031/D032 audits and to the dep map's catalog invariant.

Post-D038: simulated addition of dsl/bogus-unregistered.lisp triggers 'filesystem coverage drift (D038): dsl/bogus-unregistered.lisp exists on disk but is NOT registered as an ssot_artifact'. Tested manually + by unit test.
All 5 dsl/*.lisp and 3 ir/domain/*.ir.json files on main are registered; ssotdeps cross-check reports 'all dsl/ and ir/domain/ files registered'.
counterexamples: decisions/**/*.decision.riido.json files are NOT enumerated in the dep map (only initial-agreement is); the directory itself is the registry walked by decision validate. Different SSOT pattern - D038 does not double-check.
tools/<name>/main.go files have their own inverse check via D033 (workflow-tool coverage); D038 leaves tools/ alone.
Non-.lisp under dsl/ (e.g. README.md) and non-.ir.json under ir/domain/ (e.g. notes.txt) are intentionally out of scope - only canonical source extensions are required to be registered.
Missing dirs (fresh repo without dsl/ or ir/domain/ yet) produce no errors - the rule fires on EXISTING files, not on directory presence.
